summary: We are in a high-velocity sprint to launch the China Going-Global report and new program campaigns, with SEO prioritization shifting to Asia Star and Intelligent China.
summaryDetail: The top priority is the joint report with IE Business School on Chinese companies going global to Southeast Asia, targeting a mid-September launch at Singapore events (Tech in Asia/Tyson). Felix is drafting the one-pager, Julie is sourcing op-ed contributors and alumni case studies, and the team is translating the Chinese report. This week is critical: we need to finalize the report structure, secure contributors, and align on the launch event. Simultaneously, the Asia Start campaign is launching with two routes (AI and traditional), and the Intelligent China program website needs finalization. The SEO audit is prioritizing these two programs over US partnerships. Urgent actions include expediting the social media agency selection (considering Media Minds), finalizing the Shun Tian Shu press release (single release with 10 points), and sending the Tech in Asia proposal to Zhang Shouhuo. Upcoming: Q2/Q3 planning review and homepage messaging feedback are due this week.

meetings.title: SEO Strategy and Program Prioritization
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to improve search ranking for China-focused executive programs, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on menu categorization, school brochure updates, CES quotes for social media, Ding Hight Hall video, Asia Start articles, Davos press release, and social media agency search. Decided to streamline Shun Tian Shu press release and expedite agency selection.

meetings.title: Joint Report Planning: Chinese Companies Going Global
meetings.summary: Team discusses co-publishing a report on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with contributions from both schools, op-eds, and case studies. Launch planned for September events in Singapore.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Reviewed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional) and discussed new 'Intelligent China' program targeting foreigners, with modules in Beijing, Shanghai, Hangzhou, and Shenzhen.

meetings.title: Europe-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on geopolitical tensions affecting European companies in China, with emphasis on de-risking and the role of companies versus governments.

decisions.text: Asia Star and Intelligent China are top priority programs for SEO and marketing.
US partnerships (Stanford, Columbia) are secondary; Berkeley may be considered third priority.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu, either incorporating 10 points or converting to 10-point article.
Prioritize speed over finding the most ideal social media agency; consider Media Minds.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE Business School.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning: Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning: Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: Report will include a main research feature, op-eds from both schools, and industry case studies.
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning: Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: Target launch in mid-September at events in Singapore (Tech in Asia and/or Tyson).
decisions.meetingTitle: Joint Report Planning: Chinese Companies Going Global

decisions.text: Asia Start will run two parallel routes for the first month: AI (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy).
Intelligent China program will be marketed as a deeper dive compared to Asia Start to avoid cannibalization.
Do not confirm the brochure quote with Dean Xiang again to avoid confusion with Global Programs brochure.
decisions.meetingTitle: Re: 答复: 答复: 答复: CKGSB School Brochure (长江商学院学院英文介绍手册)_June 2026
